differential_diagnoses: Extreme insulin resistance due to anti-insulin receptor antibodies
next_steps: Measure serum insulin and proinsulin levels to evaluate for endogenous hyperinsulinemia vs exogenous insulin administration
Consider genetic testing for lipodystrophy-associated genes (e.g., PPARG, PLIN1, AGPAT2, BSCL2)
Perform a 24-hour urine free cortisol or late-night salivary cortisol to rule out Cushing's
Evaluate for underlying autoimmune or hematologic disease (e.g., complete rheumatologic workup, evaluation of bone marrow findings)
Transition to a supervised inpatient insulin protocol with strict glucose monitoring to rule out factitious behavior or malabsorption issues
